payload.text: Positioning: the only coffee subscription built around deep-work rituals

— curated by focus scientists, delivered weekly.
ICP: remote knowledge workers, 28-42, $90k+, who treat their home office as a performance environment.

artifact.positioning: The only coffee subscription built around deep-work rituals, curated by focus scientists and delivered weekly.
artifact.icp: Remote knowledge workers, 28-42, household income $90k+, who treat their home office as a performance environment.
artifact.jtbd: Give me a reliable ritual that signals the start of focused work without the distraction of deciding what to brew.
three_risks: Commoditisation risk: Blue Bottle and Trade already occupy premium ground.
Churn at month 3 when novelty fades and the subscription feels routine.
Logistics: single-origin freshness windows demand tight roast-to-ship SLAs.

payload.quality_critique: Add a quantified market size estimate to strengthen the positioning section.

payload.text: Generating brand names anchored in focus, ritual, and craft...
Top candidates: Deepbrew, Focused Origin, The Flow Roast, Meridian Coffee Co, Stillground.

names.name: Deepbrew
names.rationale: Merges deep work with brewing — memorable and direct.

names.name: Focused Origin
names.rationale: Premium single-origin signal plus the focus positioning.

names.name: The Flow Roast
names.rationale: Taps flow-state language familiar to the ICP.

names.name: Meridian Coffee Co
names.rationale: Meridian connotes peak performance and a geographic craft feel.

names.name: Stillground
names.rationale: Evokes stillness (focus) and grounds (coffee) in one word.

payload.quality_critique: Include trademark clearance notes for the top two candidates.

payload.text: Palette direction: deep espresso brown, morning mist slate, a single accent in warm amber...
Typography: Space Grotesk for headings (technical yet warm), Lora for body (editorial authority).

artifact.logoDescription: Wordmark 'Deepbrew' in Space Grotesk Medium, with a minimal hourglass-and-bean mark to the left rendered in amber on espresso.
artifact.uiNotes: Cards use 12px radius, 1px warm-grey borders, and a subtle paper-texture CSS noise overlay at 4% opacity.

payload.quality_critique: Provide a dark-mode variant palette to round out the design system.

payload.text:
Hero: "Your best work deserves better coffee."
Sub: Deepbrew ships weekly micro-lots roasted within 48 hours of dispatch.
Features: Flow-matched roast profiles / Zero decision fatigue / Freshness guarantee.

hero.headline: Your best work deserves better coffee.
hero.subheadline: Deepbrew ships weekly micro-lots roasted within 48 hours of dispatch, calibrated to your focus schedule.

features.title: Flow-matched roast profiles
features.body: Light, medium, or dark — matched to morning ramp-up, midday sprint, or afternoon recovery.

features.title: Zero decision fatigue
features.body: Answer three questions once. We handle every subsequent Friday delivery.

features.title: 48-hour freshness guarantee
features.body: Roasted Tuesday, shipped Wednesday, at your door Thursday. Or your next bag is free.

faq.a: Yes — skip or pause any week from your dashboard with no penalty.
Whole bean by default. Select your grind on signup.
Roast date is printed on every bag. Most arrive 2-3 days post-roast.

payload.quality_critique: Add a social-proof line — a real customer quote would convert 12-15% better than the current anonymous benefits list.

payload.text: Drafting launch posts for X, Product Hunt, and Hacker News...
X thread hook: "We built a coffee subscription for people who are serious about deep work. Here's the science behind the roast profiles."

twitter.hook: We built a coffee subscription for people who are serious about deep work.
thread: Roast profiles mapped to cognitive load phases.
Freshness as a feature, not a footnote.
Try Deepbrew — first bag ships Friday.

product_hunt.tagline: Weekly micro-lot coffee calibrated to your focus schedule.
product_hunt.description: Deepbrew matches roast profiles to morning ramp-up, midday sprint, and afternoon recovery. No decisions — three questions, then we handle every Friday delivery.

hacker_news.title: Show HN: Deepbrew - coffee subscription with roast profiles tied to cognitive performance research
hacker_news.comment: Built this after realising I was making 4 different coffee choices per day. ICP is remote workers who already optimise sleep, diet, and light — but never thought about caffeine timing.

payload.quality_critique: The HN title is borderline too long; trim to under 80 characters for better click-through.

payload.text: Scaffolding Next.js landing page with Tailwind, Stripe Checkout integration...
Deploying to Vercel. Build completed in 23s. Preview URL incoming.

payload.quality_critique: Add a robots.txt and sitemap.xml to the deploy — missing for SEO baseline.

payload.text: Identifying 10 warm prospects from public-record sources: Twitter/X bios, LinkedIn headlines, Product Hunt comments...
Prioritised by: stated remote work + posted about focus/productivity + active in last 30 days.

prospects.signal: Posts 3x/week about deep work, mentions Chemex in bio.
prospects.outreachAngle: Mention her recent thread on morning routines.

prospects.signal: Remote developer, pinned tweet is a 4-hour Pomodoro log.
UX lead at fully-remote SaaS, follows James Clear.
prospects.outreachAngle: Connect over habit-stacking; coffee as an anchor habit.

prospects.signal: Indie hacker, Product Hunt top hunter, espresso photo weekly.
prospects.outreachAngle: Offer early-backer pricing in exchange for a PH vote.

prospects.signal: Digital nomad, posts co-working setups, complaints about local coffee.
prospects.outreachAngle: Position as the one constant in a nomadic setup.

prospects.signal: Senior engineer at distributed team, advocates async culture.
prospects.outreachAngle: Async-work identity — coffee that respects your calendar.

prospects.signal: Productivity coach, runs 'Deep Work Fridays' newsletter.
prospects.outreachAngle: Affiliate partnership pitch — aligns with her brand.

prospects.signal: Remote-first advocate, quotes Cal Newport weekly.
prospects.outreachAngle: Newport's 'ritual' framing maps perfectly to the brand.

prospects.signal: Staff engineer, works from home office she designed herself.
prospects.outreachAngle: Home office as performance environment — she already believes it.

prospects.signal: Async manifesto author, 22k followers, mentions V60 in bio.
prospects.outreachAngle: Collab on a 'async-morning-ritual' piece — mutual reach.

payload.quality_critique: Add follower counts and engagement rates to let the founder prioritise outreach order.

payload.text: Drafting Terms of Service and Privacy Policy for subscription e-commerce, US-based entity...
Key clauses: automatic renewal disclosure (ROSCA-compliant), easy cancellation, CCPA data rights.

artifact.terms_summary: Standard e-commerce terms with ROSCA-compliant auto-renewal disclosure, 30-day refund window, and limitation of liability capped at 12 months of subscription fees.
artifact.privacy_summary: CCPA and GDPR-aligned. Data collected: email, shipping address, payment token (Stripe-held). No sale of personal data. Right to delete honored within 30 days.
flags: Subscription auto-renewal must be disclosed at checkout in bold, above the CTA — Stripe Checkout handles this natively.
California requires a dedicated 'Do Not Sell My Personal Information' link in the footer.
If shipping to EU: add a GDPR-compliant cookie banner and data processing addendum for Stripe.

payload.quality_critique: The flags section should cite specific statute sections (e.g. Cal. Civ. Code 1750) for credibility with legal reviewers.

payload.text: Running competitor teardown: Trade Coffee, Atlas Coffee Club, Blue Bottle, Mistobox...
No competitor explicitly targets the focus/productivity angle. White space confirmed.

competitors.name: Trade Coffee
competitors.weakness: No focus/productivity angle; broad lifestyle positioning.

competitors.name: Atlas Coffee Club
competitors.weakness: Novelty over ritual; churn likely at month 4-6.

competitors.name: Blue Bottle
competitors.weakness: Brand, not outcomes; no behavioural science hook.

artifact.whitespace: No competitor owns the 'deep work ritual' frame. Science-backed roast profiles + zero-decision UX is unoccupied.
artifact.price_rationale: Premium to Blue Bottle ($22) is justified by the performance positioning; target $24/mo at 250g.

payload.quality_critique: Include market size TAM/SAM/SOM figures to complete the investment narrative.

payload.text: Synthesising outputs from all nine specialists into a coherent brand narrative...

Coherence assessment: strong alignment between strategist positioning, copywriter hero, and analyst white space. Minor voice inconsistency between marketer's X thread and copywriter's landing copy — resolved below.

artifact.one_line_pitch: Deepbrew is a weekly micro-lot coffee subscription that matches roast profiles to deep-work phases, so remote workers never waste a focus window on the wrong brew.
artifact.brand_voice: Precise, calm, evidence-led. Never aspirational fluff — always a specific mechanism or outcome.
artifact.hot_take: The market is full of 'discover new coffees' subscriptions. Deepbrew wins by being the opposite: the last coffee decision you ever make.
next_actions: Spin up Stripe billing and point the Vercel deploy to a real Stripe product.
DM @ben_async — highest-leverage outreach given his audience and V60 identity.
Record a 90-second Loom of the ordering flow for the Product Hunt launch.

payload.quality_critique: The hot take is strong, but add a risk acknowledgment sentence to pre-empt the obvious skeptic question about commoditisation.
